finding_results.notes: Three polished summation lines replaced with messier, scene-pressured speech at all three cited locations. (1) Line 111: rhetorical tricolon 'That's not who you are. That's never been who you are.' replaced with rougher 'You're not. You know you're not.' — shorter, more confrontational, loses the anaphoric pattern. (2) Line 211: standalone maxim 'Not choosing is still choosing' replaced with 'You chose this, Gavin. You keep choosing it. Every night you go down there instead of — that's you choosing.' — escalating repetition of 'chose/choosing' per Miren's profile (repetitions escalate, same point with more force), self-interruption at 'instead of —' (conscious decision to stop per her sentence_completion_style), no quotable aphorism. (3) Line 217: clean conditional summation 'But if you're never actually here, that doesn't do anyone any good' replaced with 'But you're not here, Gavin. You're sorry and you're not here, and I don't — I don't know what that does for anyone.' — messy repetition of 'not here', faltered restart at 'I don't —', ends in genuine uncertainty rather than thesis-statement. All replacements maintain Miren's directness and specificity while removing authored-summation cadence. No standalone maxim remains in the form of 'Not choosing is still choosing' or equivalent quotable sentence.

summary: Single finding (chapter_14_dialogue_002) addressed across three edit sites. Miren's confrontation dialogue roughened at lines 111, 211, and 217 to replace polished thesis-lines and aphorisms with messier, pressure-true speech that interrupts itself, repeats with escalation, and lands imperfectly. The explicit maxim 'Not choosing is still choosing' is removed. All revisions are local to the cited spans. Heading contract preserved. No continuity changes introduced. Character voice profiles honored — Miren's directness, escalating repetition, and conscious self-interruption patterns maintained per style bible.
